name: Moorooduc
intro: Moorooduc is the quiet, slightly eastern edge of the Peninsula's wine country — closer to the railway that still occasionally runs steam trains on weekends than to the ridge road, and far enough off the tourist loop that a morning here feels almost private. The vineyards are smaller, the landscape flatter, the soils different enough to grow serious chardonnay that people outside the Peninsula still underrate. A handful of committed producers — Moorooduc Estate, Barmah Park, Onannon in the next village across — have turned this pocket into one of the most interesting single-vineyard collections in the state. Use Moorooduc for a slow morning cellar-door run before heading up to Red Hill for lunch.

heroImage.alt: Morning light on Moorooduc vineyard rows with low hills behind
